page: Amazon Web Services x8g.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores utilizing the 64-bit Arm architecture (arm64) with AWS Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, but provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the x8g.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core capabilities, scoring in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory performance is a primary strength, with top-tier results in both small-block cached read bandwidth (21,320.9 GB/sec) and large-block uncached read/write bandwidth. Database and transactional performance are also highly competitive, as evidenced by top-tier scores in Redis SET operations and Passmark database operations.

While multi-threaded and memory-bound operations excel, single-core performance is moderate, placing in the middle 50% for stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core tests. Additionally, the lack of x86-specific vector extensions (such as AVX or AMX) and the absence of local NVMe storage represent notable architectural tradeoffs. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the custom Arm-based Neoverse-V2 architecture delivers high resource density and exceptional memory bandwidth per core, making it an efficient option for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading.

This instance is highly suited for large-scale memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases like Redis, enterprise database operations, large-scale software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.48xlarge is an Arm-based, memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 192 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 3072.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. While the instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read/write bandwidth, and outstanding database transaction speeds, though single-core performance remains average. The absence of x86 vector extensions like AVX is an architectural tradeoff. This instance is highly suited for in-memory databases, large-scale software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.48xlarge is an Arm-based, memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated Neoverse-V2 cores, 3072 GB of RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, built for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.48xlarge is a memory-optimized Arm server with 192 dedicated cores, 3072 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: An Arm-based, memory-optimized server featuring massive memory bandwidth and dedicated physical cores for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Neoverse-V2 physical cores on arm64 architecture
3072.0 GB of system memory providing 16.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for in-memory databases, Redis, and software compilation
Lacks local storage, GPU acceleration, and x86 vector extensions
